assertions.reason: The test/CI governance review document exists and includes the required test inventory, gate classification, usefulness assessment, and missing-gates analysis for the governance-review contract.

evidence.observed: The file contains `## Test inventory`, `## CI workflow inventory`, `## Gate classification`, `## Usefulness assessment`, and `## Missing gates and weak controls`, matching the contract sections for inventory, gate classification, usefulness, and missing controls.

The narrative explicitly inventories unit, integration, truthsuite, CODE-RED, E2E, regression, smoke, performance, and standalone contract-style tests, then classifies `knowledgebase-lint`, `architecture-check`, `gate-fast`, `gate-core`, `gate-release`, `gate-reconciliation`, `gate-quality`, Checkstyle, and Qodana by effective posture.
A read-only validation script confirmed the workflow defines `gate-fast`, `gate-core`, `gate-release`, `gate-reconciliation`, and `gate-quality`, which aligns with the review document's CI gate map.

assertions.reason: The static-analysis triage document classifies the backlog by severity bucket, type group, and rule concentration hotspots as required.

evidence.observed: The file contains `## Severity buckets`, `## Type groups`, and `## Rule concentration hotspots`, including the required severity buckets (`blocker`, `critical`, `major`, `minor`, `info`) and type groups (`security`, `correctness`, `nullability`, `performance`, `maintainability`, `style`).

The hotspot tables identify concentration candidates such as `AccountingCoreEngineCore.java`, `SalesCoreEngine.java`, `AccountingServiceTest.java`, and other large files, satisfying the contract's rule-concentration requirement.

A read-only validation script confirmed that Qodana thresholds (`critical: 0`, `any: 50`) and advisory Checkstyle configuration (`failsOnError=false`, warning severity) match the triage document's posture summary.

assertions.reason: The static-analysis triage document defines a baseline-first, new-violations-only quality gate that blocks new high-risk issues while tolerating legacy debt temporarily.

evidence.observed: The file contains `## Quality gate strategy`, `### Baseline policy`, and `### New-violations-only`, satisfying the contract's required sections.

The recommended gate rules explicitly say new blocker and security findings fail immediately, new critical correctness/nullability findings fail, hotspot-package majors are elevated, and legacy minor/info findings are baseline noise.

The practical rollout sequence says to capture a baseline, wire Qodana into CI, filter Checkstyle to changed files or baseline deltas, and fail only on new high-risk findings first, which matches the contract's baseline-policy and new-violations-only requirements.

frictions: Task delegation to `user-testing-flow-validator` failed with `Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-xhigh`, so this docs-only validation group was executed directly in-session per `.factory/library/user-testing.md`.

Passive runtime setup probes could not connect to `127.0.0.1:8081`, `http://localhost:9090/actuator/health`, or `127.0.0.1:5433` during this round, but the assigned governance-review assertions remained fully testable from `docs/code-review/**`.
